content.problemStatement:
Chef is a big fan of Coldplay. Every Sunday, he will drive to a park taking MMM minutes to reach there, and during the ride he will play a single song on a loop. Today, he has got the latest song which is in total SSS minutes long. He is interested to know how many times will he be able to play the song completely.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The only line of each test case contains two space-separated integers M,SM, SM,S - the duration of the trip and the duration of the song, both in minutes.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line the answer to the problem.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers M and S — the duration of the trip in minutes and the duration of the song in minutes.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the maximum number of complete times the song can be played during the trip.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: 10/5 = 2 complete plays.\nTest case 2: 10/6 = 1 complete play (the remaining 4 minutes are insufficient).\nTest case 3: 9/10 = 0 because the song is longer than the trip.

solution.approach: For each test case the answer is simply the integer division of the trip duration by the song length, i.e., floor(M / S). This can be computed directly using the '/' operator for integers in most languages.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int T; if(scanf("%d",&T)!=1) return 0; while(T--){ long long M,S; scanf("%lld %lld",&M,&S); printf("%lld\n", M/S); } return 0; }
